description_long: This object consists of three reddish-brown rectangular blocks stacked in a staggered arrangement, resembling standard construction bricks. Each block features a series of evenly spaced circular indentations on the top surface, typical of perforated bricks used in masonry. The blocks have a rough, matte texture, suggesting they are made from fired clay or concrete. The edges are straight and well-defined, with no visible chips or cracks, and the surfaces appear solid and uniform in color. The gaps between the blocks create small voids, but the overall structure is compact and stable. This configuration is commonly seen in temporary barriers or construction sites within urban environments.
description: A stack of three reddish-brown perforated stone blocks with rough, matte surfaces and circular indentations on top, arranged in a staggered formation. The blocks are solid and uniform, resembling construction bricks.
description_view_0: Front view showing three stacked blocks with visible circular indentations and a central gap between the lower blocks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the staggered arrangement and the overhanging top block, with indentations visible on the upper surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the aligned backs of the blocks and the uniformity of the surfaces, with less visible indentations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the offset stacking and the open space beneath the top block.
attribute_location_context: urban construction site or temporary street barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and edges are sharp, indicating minimal exposure to the elements.
